content.problemStatement:
This problem has subtasks.

NNN football players stand in a circle, uniformly separated.

The distance between two players is the minimum number of players between them in either the clockwise or anticlockwise direction, plus 111.

That is, the distance between players iii and jjj is d(i,j)=min‚Å°(‚à£i‚àíj‚à£,N‚àí‚à£i‚àíj‚à£)d(i, j) = \min(|i-j|, N-|i-j|)d(i,j)=min(‚à£i‚àíj‚à£,N‚àí‚à£i‚àíj‚à£).

As part of their passing drill, these players must complete **exactly** MMM passes.

When a player has the ball, he'll pass as follows:


You're given an array WWW of length ‚åäN2‚åã\left\lfloor \frac{N}{2} \right\rfloor‚åä2N‚Äã‚åã.

It is guaranteed that at least one element of WWW is positive.

Let S=sum(W)S = sum(W)S=sum(W).
The player with the ball will first choose a random distance from 111 to ‚åäN2‚åã\left\lfloor \frac{N}{2} \right\rfloor‚åä2N‚Äã‚åã.

The probability of choosing distance ddd is WdS\frac{W_d}{S}SWd‚Äã‚Äã.
Then, he will pass the ball to whichever player is at distance ddd from him.

If there are multiple players at distance ddd, one of them will be chosen uniformly at random.

When player xxx passes to player yyy, it will be done along the straight line segment connecting them inside the circle.
For a fixed parameter kkk, a Tiki Taka is a **continuous** sequence of kkk passes that satisfies the following conditions:


Let the sequence of players who had the ball be P1,P2,‚Ä¶,Pk,Pk+1P_1, P_2, \ldots, P_k, P_{k+1}P1‚Äã,P2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Pk‚Äã,Pk+1‚Äã, where player P1P_1P1‚Äã had the ball initially and for each 1‚â§i‚â§k1 \leq i \leq k1‚â§i‚â§k, PiP_iPi‚Äã passes to Pi+1P_{i+1}Pi+1‚Äã.
P1=Pk+1P_1 = P_{k+1}P1‚Äã=Pk+1‚Äã should hold, i.e, the ball should return to the one who started passing.

Apart from that Pi‚âÝPjP_i \neq P_jPi‚ÄãÓÄÝ=Pj‚Äã for any i‚âÝji\neq jiÓÄÝ=j.
During this sequence of kkk passes, the ball never touches the same point inside the circle twice.
There exists at least one player (say xxx) such that the line segment joining xxx and the center of the circle **does not intersect** the path of the ball across all kkk passes.

In particular, xxx cannot be one of the PiP_iPi‚Äã.

Further, note that if the ball itself passes through the center during the passes, this condition cannot hold.
For example, suppose players numbered 111 to 888 are standing anticlockwise in a circle, spaced equally. Then,


The sequence of passes 1‚Üí4‚Üí5‚Üí2‚Üí11 \rightarrow 4 \rightarrow 5 \rightarrow 2 \rightarrow 11‚Üí4‚Üí5‚Üí2‚Üí1 is not a Tiki Taka: the ball crosses its own path.
The sequence of passes 1‚Üí2‚Üí3‚Üí2‚Üí11 \rightarrow 2 \rightarrow 3 \rightarrow 2 \rightarrow 11‚Üí2‚Üí3‚Üí2‚Üí1 is not a Tiki Taka: player 222 passes twice.
The sequence of passes 1‚Üí3‚Üí6‚Üí11 \rightarrow 3 \rightarrow 6 \rightarrow 11‚Üí3‚Üí6‚Üí1 is not a Tiki Taka: there's no player such that the segment joining them to the center doesn't intersect the ball's path.
The sequence of passes 1‚Üí2‚Üí3‚Üí11 \rightarrow 2 \rightarrow 3 \rightarrow 11‚Üí2‚Üí3‚Üí1 is a Tiki Taka, if k=3k = 3k=3.
The score of a sequence of MMM passes is the number of its contiguous subsequences that are Tiki Takas.

You are given N,M,kN, M, kN,M,k, and WWW.

Find the expected score of a sequence of MMM passes, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.

Formally, it can be shown that the required expected value can be written in its lowest form as PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP‚Äã, where PPP and QQQ are integers, and gcd‚Å°(Q,998244353)=1\gcd(Q, 998244353) = 1gcd(Q,998244353)=1.

There will exist a unique integer RRR (0‚â§R<9982443530 \leq R \lt 9982443530‚â§R<998244353) such that P‚â°Q‚ãÖR(mod998244353)P \equiv Q\cdot R \pmod {998244353}P‚â°Q‚ãÖR(mod998244353). Find this integer RRR.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and kkk ‚Äî the number of players, passes and the number of continuous passes in a sequence used to determine a Tiki Taka.
The next line contains ‚åäN2‚åã\left\lfloor \frac{N}{2} \right\rfloor‚åä2N‚Äã‚åã integers, the elements of array WWW.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the expected score the squad will be awarded, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: It is guaranteed that the sum of WiW_iWi‚Äã is not a multiple of 998244353998244353998244353.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.
**Subtask 111 (202020 points):** The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 500050005000.
